title: Pilosella lagopus
intro: Pilosella lagopus, also known as Mouse-ear Hawk's-beard, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) with a characteristic hairy appearance. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, inflammation, and fever, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella lagopus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or inflorescence extract, and is used to treat conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome, bronchitis, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Pilosella lagopus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants commonly known as wood-sorrels. Within the genus Pilosella, Pilosella lagopus is considered to be a member of the Pilosella caespitosa complex. This complex is a group of closely related species that are often difficult to distinguish from one another. The classification of Pilosella lagopus is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of plant taxonomy.
uses: Pilosella lagopus, also known as rabbit's foot or false dandelion, has been used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory properties. constituents: Pilosella lagopus contains a range of active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, such as pilosulin and lagoposide. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
